Excessive number of failed connections from 3.1.128.83,CAM-11488,380866,[System] Incident,Closed,CAM,Chartered Asset Management Pte Ltd,service_desk,Logicalis GSOC,611ca5f3aee32f006f99cf9d,,https://logicalisasia.atlassian.net/servicedesk/customer/portal/4,Low,Done,Muhammad Zahid Bin Saiful Adlan,712020:377b188e-bd69-4a61-8da1-6e49d1837be1,Logicalis GSOC,611ca5f3aee32f006f99cf9d,Logicalis GSOC,611ca5f3aee32f006f99cf9d,28/2/2026 23:46,1/3/2026 1:26,,1/3/2026 1:26,,0,suspicious-activity,,"The client at address 3.1.128.83 generated more than 5000 failures over a 5 minutes time window, which may indicate malicious activity.",,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,com.atlassian.servicedesk.plugins.approvals.internal.customfield.ApprovalsCFValue@4f900e00,,,rgandhiraj,638ffec99e48f2b9a6168b6d,,,,Benign Positive,,28/2/2026 23:46,,,,,,,"Accounts:
The network team has checked and advised the following

“Based on our review of the provided information, this traffic has been denied by the FortiGate firewall for a long time under the default implicit deny rule. No action is required from our end at this time.”
The source IP address *3.1.128.83* does not belong to your environment.”
We have checked and confirmed that destination IP 118.201.196.188 is no longer in use.
We will inform our ISP to remove the route after we have reviewed the rest of IP addresses within the subnet provided by the ISP.

The network team has checked and advised the following

We will inform our ISP to remove the route after we have reviewed the rest of IP addresses within the subnet provided by the ISP.

column1: Anomalous sign-in location by user account and authenticating application,CAM-11452,373476,[System] Incident,Closed,CAM,Chartered Asset Management Pte Ltd,service_desk,Logicalis GSOC,611ca5f3aee32f006f99cf9d,,https://logicalisasia.atlassian.net/servicedesk/customer/portal/4,Medium,Done,Keegan Tang,712020:a0da00ef-dff0-4df4-a9ff-96b5a2033a2e,Logicalis GSOC,611ca5f3aee32f006f99cf9d,Logicalis GSOC,611ca5f3aee32f006f99cf9d,19/2/2026 17:00,19/2/2026 17:16,,19/2/2026 17:16,,0,InitialAccess,,"This query over Azure Active Directory sign-in considers all user sign-ins for each Azure Active
Directory application and picks out the most anomalous change in location profile for a user within an
individual application. An alert is generated for recent sign-ins that have location counts that are anomalous
over last day but also over the last 3-day and 7-day periods.
Please note that on workspaces with larger volume of Signin data (~10M+ events a day) may timeout when using this default query time period.

column1: GSOC had received following Alert, please find the incident details below:

Identifies anomalous increases in Exchange mail items accessed operations. The query leverages KQL built-in anomaly detection algorithms to find large deviations from baseline patterns. Sudden increases in execution frequency of sensitive actions should be further investigated for malicious activity. Manually change scorethreshold from 1.5 to 3 or higher to reduce the noise based on outliers flagged from the query criteria.
GSOC Observed that from the user *“*[*siewkheng@cam.com.sg*
column2: mailto:siewkheng@cam.com.sg]*”* was triggered the alerts on the Exchange mail items accessed operations anomaly on *18/02/2026, 5:05:41 pm*. Please check with user if the activity was legitimate.

column1: # Kindly verify with user if the activity was legitimate.
# If the activity was not performed by the user, kindly request user to change password immediately.

I have checked this activity, and it is legitimate. Client is accessing outlook email from her iOS devices. Mail which are in specific folder or date which is not in the cache often reaches the server to retrieve those mail items and this might flag under the category.
{color:#172B4D}Please let me know if you need any further information or clarification.{color}{color:#172B4D}{color}

Power Virtual Agent is a service that allows users to create chatbots without coding. The purpose of adding an owner to an application is to grant administrative right to manage and configure the chatbot.  This activity is legitimate, as such activities are common when applications are being updated or when new features are being deployed, especially in environments where services like Power Virtual Agents are integrated with other Microsoft services. column6: *_10014_*:*_1_*:*_0,,,"16/Feb/26 5:15 PM;712020:1956e8c3-2e78-4835-bbe4-8043097cbf0b;This activity was performed automated processes or configurations. Power Virtual Agent is a service that allows users to create chatbots without coding. The purpose of adding an owner to an application is to grant administrative right to manage and configure the chatbot. The network team has checked and advised the following

“Based on our review of the provided information, this traffic has been denied by the FortiGate firewall for a long time under the default implicit deny rule. No action is required from our end at this time.”
The source IP address *3.1.128.83* does not belong to your environment.”
We have checked and confirmed that destination IP 118.201.196.188 is no longer in use.
We will inform our ISP to remove the route after we have reviewed the rest of IP addresses within the subnet provided by the ISP.

column1: A threat actor may try to modify policies to weaken the security controls in place.
Investigate any change to ensure they are approved.
GSOC had received the following alert(s), please find the incident details below:

column3: Conditional Access Policy Modified by New User

column1: * Microsoft-managed: Require phishing-resistant multifactor authentication for admins

Detects a Conditional Access Policy being modified by a user who has not modified a policy in the last 14 days. A threat actor may try to modify policies to weaken the security controls in place. Investigate any change to ensure they are approved.

GSOC had observed that the user “*lsgadmin2@cam.com.sg”* was involved in the addition of two conditional access policies as mentioned above at time *03/02/2026, 14:26:34.494 (SGT).* Please check if the activity was legitimate from the user.
# Please confirm with user that this is a legitimate activity.
# If this is a suspicious action, kindly disable the account for further investigation.
